knimeNode.name: DINOS Numeric Subgroup Parser
knimeNode.shortDescription: Create DINOS subgroups for given a dataset and a text descriptions of said subgroups

fullDescription.intro:
From textual description of subgroups, find the instances in the data that belongs to said subgroups. DINOS Parser Nodes accept both rules without the "THEN" part (which will be calculated on run time) or the full "IF-THEN". Useful for either recreating DINOS runs, evaluating arbitrary hand-made subgroups or seeing how different metrics evaluate the same subgroups.
This specific node is for use in tables with a single numeric target variable among the Integer or Double columns (not Long).Rules for this mode can be input as either:
1 - Just a String Column with the IF part: column1 = {temperature in [97.2; 97.8]}
2 - A String Column with both the IF and the "THEN" part: column1 = {"temperature in [97.2; 97.8] --> heart_rate in [58.0; 78.0]}"

tab.name: General
tab.option: The column to define as target for subgroup discovery. The node refuses to execute or open the dialogue window If it can't find a column of the supported types for this mode (Integer or Double).
The column with either the IF part of the subgroup description or the whole "IF-THEN"

tab.name: Components
tab.option: If true, ignore all custom components configuration and use the default one
Hover the mouse pointer over components to read a description of them. Also, for most use cases it makes sense to change "objectives" and "class Selector"
Method or formulas for the quality of a subgroup. Set these for when you want to know the value of a metric without it influencing in the subgroup results (for that, use "objectives").
Metrics to use to influence in the quality of subgroups. If you only want to know a value of a metric, instead set in "metrics".
Criteria to select the value of the target variable in each subgroup
Determine if a candidate subgroup is feasible
Feasibility criteria of an individual, which modifies previously defined candidate subgroups as unfeasible until it is marked as feasible
Modifies candidate subgroup to a certain criteria after creation
Dominance criteria between two candidate subgroups to use in the genetic algorithm, dominance determines which of two subgroups to be compared is the best
Redundancy criteria between two candidate subgroups to use in the genetic algorithm, which determines if the subgroups to be compared cover the same instances i.e: their results overlap each other
After each algorithm iteration, which filter to apply to determine if any of the newly generated subgroups is better than the ones previously defined as the best
An individual is an internal representation of a candidate subgroup, not final ones as presented to the user
At the beginning of each iteration, which method to use to create new candidate subgroups
Method to use to determine the best candidates among the initial population
Crossover method for the genetic algorithm, which creates a new candidate subgroup from a list of other candidates
Mutation method for the genetic algorithm, which alters the given candidate based on a certain criteria

tab.name: Settings
tab.option: The algorithm will make no more evaluations and stop after doing the specified amount. Due to the iterative cycle of the genetic algorithm where it replaces subgroups previously defined as the best as it finds better ones the higher this number is, the more chances and time the algorithm will have to find better subgroups. However, making it larger will not magically find better subgroups for the given data, there being a limit depending on both the component configuration and the distribution of the data.
The components of the algorithm can use variables to fine tune their behavior. All available settings are collected here, notice that some components may use the same setting.
Hover the mouse pointer over settings to read a description of them

ports.inPort: The table which to use as dataset
The table with subgroup descriptions for parsing
ports.outPort: Description of each instance and which subgroup it belongs
